pi_items.source: Consistent across setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and task description. Path matches system configuration for user 'ga'.
Task description specifies all 9 required layers with detailed specifications for each. Verifier.py confirms these same layer names.

Task description requires: Title 'IRRIGATION WELL DETAIL' and labels: 'GROUND SURFACE', 'STATIC WATER LEVEL', 'PUMPING WATER LEVEL', 'CASING', 'SCREEN', 'GRAVEL PACK', 'BENTONITE SEAL', 'SUBMERSIBLE PUMP'. All required keywords are present in these labels.

Task specifies total depth of 150' (Y=-150). min_depth=140 is the minimum Y-threshold in verifier.py for passing validation. Task description specifies 'Total Depth (150')', confirming the well extends to Y=-150.

IFAS (University of Florida) Ch166 standard for screened agricultural irrigation wells: 8 inches ID is optimal for wells with 200-600 gpm yield. Task specifies '8-inch casing' matching standard industrial specifications.
IFAS Ch166 standard confirms 6-inch screens are appropriate for agricultural irrigation wells. Task specifies '6-inch screen' with vertical lines at X±3.

pi_items.metadata_value: Approximately 3-4 inches (X±10 for casing at X±4)
pi_items.source: Task specifies gravel pack boundary lines at X±10 with casing at X±4, yielding ~3 inch thickness on each side. IFAS Ch166 recommends 3-8 inches of gravel pack thickness.

Industry standards allow 10-100 feet seal depth depending on jurisdiction. Task specifies 25-foot seal from ground surface, which is within recommended range. Common spec: bentonite chips 6 feet with 3 feet cement cap above.
Task description explicitly states 'Total Depth (150')'. This is a realistic depth for agricultural irrigation wells, within typical range of 100-200+ feet.
Task specifies 'Static water level line at Y=-40' (40 feet down from Y=0 ground surface). This is realistic for agricultural wells in many regions.
Task specifies 'Pumping water level line at Y=-80'. The 40-foot drawdown (from -40 to -80) is realistic for operational wells under pumping stress.

pi_items.verified_value: Task specifications are based on real USDA and IFAS agricultural irrigation well construction standards, not synthetic data
pi_items.source: IFAS Publication CH166 (University of Florida): Design and Construction of Screened Wells for Agricultural Irrigation Systems. USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 642 (Water Well). Industry standards confirm all dimensional specifications.

privileged_info_summary: The water well section task is based on real agricultural irrigation well construction standards. The 8-inch casing and 6-inch screen dimensions are verified standard configurations per IFAS Ch166 for 200-600 gpm wells. The 150-foot total depth is realistic for irrigation wells. The 25-foot bentonite seal depth is within industry-recommended range (10-100 feet depending on jurisdiction). The 40-foot static water level and 80-foot pumping level demonstrate realistic operational conditions. All 9 required layers (GROUND, CASING, SCREEN, GRAVEL, WATER, PUMP, SEAL, DIMENSIONS, TEXT) and required text keywords are consistent with engineering drawing standards for well construction details. The min_depth threshold of 140 feet is appropriate for verifying the 150-foot total depth specification.
